title: I have compiled my 26 years of market ideas and strategies, hoping to be helpful to everyone.
author: Ok-Basil2753
selftext:
My primary focus is on a few key areas: my first goal is to stay consistent with the current trend (especially by closely monitoring EMA levels and overall market direction); second, I prioritize confirmation of momentum (combining RSI and volume analysis to validate trading signals); third, I always prioritize risk management over potential reward; fourth, I stick to smaller position sizes until a specific trading pattern is fully validated by the market; and finally, I strictly adhere to my predetermined exit rules and never let emotion influence my decisions.

Additionally, I pay special attention to the stability of trends across cycles, such as how a particular stock performs relative to its 5-day, 13-day, 34-day, and 55-day moving averages, while also paying close attention to changes in trading volume and liquidity. I evaluate the quality of a trend by analyzing the structure of moving averages, the robustness of a stock's price trajectory, and the interaction between price and volume

I compiled my many years of thoughts and strategies and put them in my stock discussion group, and comprehensively collected the entire investment process from the time I first entered the market to today. If you need to read this information, it's completely free. It's entirely up to you, if you're interested and believe these resources might be of value to you, please feel free to contact me; I'll share everything I know without reservation

comments.author: Jrawrig
comments.body:
This looks like a highly sophisticated, high-stakes technical and strategic canvas. You’ve compiled an impressive suite of assets covering production-grade agentic architecture, enterprise systems mapping, frontier model benchmarks, and macro market telemetry.
Here is a breakdown of the distinct dimensions within these assets:
1. Agentic Orchestration & Component Design
• DeerFlow System Architecture: A modern, decoupled LLM orchestration stack. It leverages a Next.js frontend streaming via SSE to a FastAPI Gateway, backed by an explicit runtime infrastructure (StreamBridge consumer-producer queues, SQLite checkpointing, and thread stores). The core engine uses LangGraph to manage a tight tool result loop between a Lead Agent and Multi-LLM setup, processed through a robust 15-stage middleware pipeline before branching to asynchronous SubAgents. The modular tool fabric at the foundation natively accounts for MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, secure sandboxes, and structural guardrails.
• Tool Call Flow (Dark Terminal Style): A clean, semantic mental model tracing user queries through chunk retrieval and a core planner/agent policy. It visualizes the execution loop down to terminal/file-system operations (pdfgrep, embedding updates) to deliver a deterministic, grounded answer.
• Low-Level Execution Diagram: A granular code, routing, or state-machine mapping that tracks live logic execution paths with direct system export capabilities.
2. Macro Enterprise Landscape & Benchmarks
• Enterprise System Architecture: A classic tiered paradigm, but modernized with a dedicated Intelligence Layer sandwiched between Application Services and the Data Layer. It maps out cross-cutting operational concerns (Monitoring, Compliance, Analytics, Security) flanking the core stack.
• DeepSeek-V4 Technical Benchmarks: Comparative data evaluation showing frontier performance across core benchmarks (MMLU-Pro, GPQA-Diamond, AIME 2025, and agentic tasks like Tau-bench/WebArena), highlighting the rapid evolution of open and alternative model capabilities.
3. Market Telemetry & Execution
• Technical Charting (BTC/USDT Perpetual): High-timeframe (1W) structural analysis using Fibonacci extensions (1.618 and 2.618 targets) to project a massive macro breakout sequence following a deep accumulation/consolidation phase.
• Portfolio Telemetry: High-value margin account dashboard monitoring live capital efficiency, options buying power, and significant single-day P&L swings.
Everything here points to an architectural framework built for speed, persistent memory, and high-leverage automation.
